title: Genmab — White Space
html:
Genmab — White-Space BD Map

Last updated: 2026-04-10 AO: Dave Johnson | AD: Melissa Weinstein FY25: $1.34M | FY26 Target: $1.47M (+10%) | FY26 YTD: $482K (33%) Gap to target: $718K | Weighted pipeline: $270K Non-repeatable FY25 revenue: ~$500K (must be replaced) Vinoth's cluster: East 6 (Winston, Aruna — MSL projects; Austino — EM)

1. Brand x Service Matrix

Service Epkinly (lymphoma) RINA-S (ovarian/endometrial) Tivdak (cervical, AbbVie) DuoBody pipeline
PET ACTIVE (renewed) Pre-launch opportunity Opportunity (AbbVie budget?) Too early
ATU Opportunity Pre-launch opportunity Opportunity Too early
HCP-Patient Opportunity Pre-launch opportunity Low fit Too early
MSL Tracking ACTIVE (heme) Opportunity (solid tumor) Low fit Too early
Digital Tracking Opportunity Pre-launch opportunity Low fit Too early
Message Testing Opportunity HIGH PRIORITY Opportunity Too early
Launch KPI N/A (launched) HIGH PRIORITY N/A Too early
Qual Opportunity Pre-launch opportunity Low fit Too early
Social Listening Low fit Opportunity Low fit Too early

Legend: ACTIVE = booked/in-flight | HIGH PRIORITY = strong fit + timing | Opportunity = viable | Low fit = deprioritize

2. Top 5 Opportunities (Ranked)

# Opportunity Brand Est. Value Confidence Rationale
1 RINA-S pre-launch bundle (PET + ATU + Message Testing + Launch KPI) RINA-S $250-400K Medium-High This is THE growth engine. Late 2026 approval anticipated. Genmab will need pre-launch landscape assessment, HCP attitude baseline, message testing for launch materials, and launch KPIs. Bundle pricing makes the pitch compelling. Timing: pitch Q2 2026 for Q3-Q4 start.
2 Epkinly PET expansion (combo therapy tracking) Epkinly $50-80K High Epkinly just got combo therapy approval (Nov '25). The renewed PET should be expanded to capture combo vs. mono prescribing dynamics. Low-effort upsell.
3 RINA-S MSL Tracking (solid tumor) RINA-S $80-120K Medium Genmab already buys heme MSL Tracking. Solid tumor MSL Tracking for RINA-S is a natural extension. Winston/Aruna can deliver.
4 Epkinly Message Testing Epkinly $60-80K Medium Post-combo approval, Epkinly messaging will evolve. Message testing helps Genmab optimize positioning against Mosunetuzumab (Roche) and glofitamab.
5 Tivdak PET or ATU (AbbVie co-promote) Tivdak $60-100K Low-Medium Tricky — AbbVie co-promotes, so budget may sit with AbbVie. Requires Angela Sun (AbbVie co-promote contact) to champion internally. Worth exploring but not bankable.

Total addressable (all 5): ~$500-780K Realistic FY26 conversion: ~$300-450K — which, combined with $482K YTD, gets to $782-932K. Still short of $1.47M target unless RINA-S bundle lands at scale.

Honest assessment: The $718K gap is difficult to close. Weighted pipeline at $270K covers only 38% of the gap. RINA-S pre-launch is the swing factor — if it lands, the account hits target. If RINA-S slips to 2027 or Genmab uses a different vendor, the account will miss.

3. Competitive Context

Genmab is a bispecific antibody pioneer transitioning from a biotech to a commercial-stage pharma company. Epkinly competes directly with Roche's Mosunetuzumab and Glofitamab in lymphoma — ZoomRx PET data is critical for Genmab to track competitive share. RINA-S (with Roche partnership) entering ovarian/endometrial cancer faces competition from Elahere (ImmunoGen/AbbVie) and emerging ADCs. The Merus acquisition adds DuoBody pipeline complexity but is unlikely to generate revenue in FY26. The account transition from Brooke to Dave/Melissa creates relationship risk — Luan Dao and Angela Sun need to build trust with the new AO team. ZoomRx's incumbency on Epkinly PET and MSL Tracking is a strong competitive moat, but RINA-S pre-launch will be competitively bid.

4. Connection to AO Plan / Gap Analysis

  • $500K non-repeatable revenue is the structural problem. Even with solid renewals, Genmab needs significant new wins to stay flat, let alone grow 10%.
  • The account transition from Brooke to Dave/Melissa adds execution risk. Relationships with Luan Dao and Angela Sun need active management.
  • Dave/Melissa must own the RINA-S pre-launch pitch — this is too large and strategic for delivery teams to lead.
  • Vinoth's East 6 (Winston, Aruna) owns MSL delivery. Their work quality on the heme MSL project is the proof point that gets Genmab to buy RINA-S solid tumor MSL Tracking.
  • Weighted pipeline at $270K is dangerously low. The team needs to generate $400-500K more pipeline in the next 60 days or this account will miss target.

5. Recommended Next Actions for Vinoth

  1. Push Dave/Melissa to pitch RINA-S pre-launch bundle NOW (April 2026). Late 2026 approval means Genmab's pre-launch planning is happening this quarter. If ZoomRx isn't in the conversation by May, someone else will be. This is urgent.
  2. Ensure Winston and Aruna deliver exceptional MSL Tracking work. The heme MSL project is the live audition for RINA-S MSL Tracking. Any quality issues here directly kill the #3 opportunity.
  3. Brief Melissa on Epkinly combo expansion angle. Prepare a short pitch: "Your PET was designed pre-combo approval. The landscape has changed. Here's what an expanded tracker looks like." Easy win, fast close.
  4. Help Dave/Melissa navigate the AO transition. Offer to join a relationship-building call with Luan Dao or Angela Sun. Vinoth's delivery credibility through East 6 can accelerate trust-building.
  5. Explore Merus acquisition implications. Ask Melissa to find out if the Merus pipeline creates new insights needs. This is a 2027+ play but planting the seed now is free.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
